title: Project And Program Management
summary: Use AI to improve planning, risk management, stakeholder updates, and recurring coordination work.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: AI As A Project Co-Pilot
lessons.subtitle: Use AI to support planning and communication, not replace project judgment.
lessons.objective: Identify where AI adds leverage in the project workflow and where human decisions still dominate.
lessons.useWhen: Use AI to support planning and communication, not replace project judgment.
lessons.whyItMatters: Project work mixes structure and judgment. AI is strong at organizing inputs and drafting outputs, but weak at owning tradeoffs.

coreConcepts.title: Use AI For Structure
coreConcepts.text: Planning outlines, risk lists, recap drafts, and status formats are all strong fits.

coreConcepts.title: Keep Humans On Tradeoffs
coreConcepts.text: Priority, scope, staffing, and escalation still require human authority.

coreConcepts.title: Anchor In Real Project Artifacts
coreConcepts.text: Briefs, timelines, issue logs, and meeting notes make AI output much more reliable.

workedExample.scenario: A PM needs to turn a rough initiative brief into a kickoff plan.
workedExample.approach: They provide the brief, milestones, constraints, and stakeholders, then ask AI for a planning skeleton.
workedExample.result: The first draft is more structured and easier to refine.
workedExample.verification: The PM checks scope, dependency assumptions, and resource implications before sharing.

doThisNow.task: Identify three PM tasks you can accelerate safely with AI this week.
steps: List what still needs human review.

commonMistakes: Asking AI to make project decisions alone.
Planning from memory instead of artifacts.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Plan From A Brief
lessons.subtitle: Good plans start with clear goals, boundaries, and assumptions.
lessons.objective: Use AI to draft project plans and milestone structures from an existing brief.
lessons.useWhen: Good plans start with clear goals, boundaries, and assumptions.
lessons.whyItMatters: Project plans improve when AI helps surface missing assumptions and organize tasks into a clearer sequence.

coreConcepts.title: Start With The Goal
coreConcepts.text: Define the outcome, deadline, and constraints before asking for tasks.

coreConcepts.title: Surface Assumptions
coreConcepts.text: Plans are only as good as the assumptions behind them. Ask AI to identify what is unknown or unstated.

coreConcepts.title: Use Reviewable Structure
coreConcepts.text: Ask for phases, milestones, dependencies, and risks instead of one long prose plan.

workedExample.scenario: A program manager receives a vague internal request with a target quarter.
workedExample.approach: They use AI to turn the brief into milestones, open assumptions, and a draft dependency list.
workedExample.result: The conversation shifts from vague ambition to a concrete planning discussion.
workedExample.verification: The PM checks that milestone dates and dependencies align with actual team capacity.

doThisNow.task: Use one active brief to generate a milestone-based project outline.
steps: Review the draft with actual constraints in mind.

commonMistakes: Using AI without a real brief.
Sharing the plan before checking feasibility.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use AI For Risk Reviews
lessons.subtitle: Risk identification improves when you ask from multiple perspectives.
lessons.objective: Generate better risk lists and mitigation options using structured prompts.
lessons.useWhen: Risk identification improves when you ask from multiple perspectives.
lessons.whyItMatters: Teams often under-scan risk because they focus on delivery tasks and ignore stakeholder, resource, or change-management issues.

coreConcepts.title: Prompt From Different Lenses
coreConcepts.text: Schedule, resource, dependency, stakeholder, and adoption lenses reveal different categories of risk.

coreConcepts.title: Ask For Mitigation, Not Just Lists
coreConcepts.text: A useful risk review includes early warning signals and response options.

coreConcepts.title: Separate Probability From Impact
coreConcepts.text: A ranked list is more useful than a brainstormed pile.

workedExample.scenario: A project has a strong delivery plan but weak adoption planning.
workedExample.approach: The PM asks AI to review the plan from delivery, stakeholder, and change-management perspectives.
workedExample.result: New risks appear that were invisible in the first draft.
workedExample.verification: The PM validates which risks are real versus generic and adds only those supported by the project context.

doThisNow.task: Run a two-lens risk review on one project you own.

commonMistakes: Accepting generic mitigation advice without context.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Write Faster Status Updates
lessons.subtitle: Status work is easier when AI organizes the raw material first.
lessons.objective: Create faster, clearer status reports from existing project notes and trackers.
lessons.useWhen: Status work is easier when AI organizes the raw material first.
lessons.whyItMatters: Status reporting is repetitive and format-driven, making it a strong fit for AI support.

coreConcepts.title: Collect The Inputs
coreConcepts.text: Issue logs, milestone status, notes, and blockers should feed the draft.

coreConcepts.title: Lead With What Changed
coreConcepts.text: Good status updates highlight movement, risk, and decisions needed.

coreConcepts.title: Tailor For The Reader
coreConcepts.text: Leadership, peers, and working teams want different levels of detail.

workedExample.scenario: A PM spends 45 minutes every week consolidating updates.
workedExample.approach: They use AI to turn the tracker and notes into a status draft with a fixed format.
workedExample.result: The reporting time drops and the message becomes clearer.
workedExample.verification: The PM checks that the report reflects the current tracker, not old thread context.

doThisNow.task: Use a current tracker and notes to draft one status report.
steps: Verify the dates and risks before sending.

commonMistakes: Using the same detail level for every audience.
Letting AI hide bad news in polished prose.

roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Build A PM Prompt Library
lessons.subtitle: Store the patterns that help recurring project work move faster.
lessons.objective: Capture reusable prompts and formats for planning, risk review, and status updates.
lessons.useWhen: Store the patterns that help recurring project work move faster.
lessons.whyItMatters: Project work repeats. Saving what works reduces rework and makes team norms more consistent.

coreConcepts.title: Save Prompt + Artifact
coreConcepts.text: Keep the prompt alongside the input pattern and output format.

coreConcepts.title: Document Review Rules
coreConcepts.text: Record what must be checked before sharing the draft.

coreConcepts.title: Version The Pattern
coreConcepts.text: Update the prompt when you learn a better way to frame the work.

workedExample.scenario: A PM creates a planning prompt that works well but cannot remember why.
workedExample.approach: They save the prompt, the planning brief structure, and the review notes.
workedExample.result: The next project kickoff starts from a proven asset instead of a blank page.
workedExample.verification: They test the saved pattern on a second project before adopting it more broadly.

doThisNow.task: Save one effective PM prompt as a reusable library entry.

commonMistakes: Not retesting the prompt on another project.

outcomes: Use AI as a project planning and reporting co-pilot.
Surface assumptions, risks, and status faster.
nextModuleBridge: Next, use AI for data interpretation and business analysis without losing rigor.
